title: What Can Generative Models Create?
concept: Generative models can produce brand-new faces, artwork, product photos, and even synthetic training data to help fix imbalanced datasets — as long as they've learned the pattern of a training set well enough to sample fresh, plausible examples from it.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What question does a generative model answer, unlike a discriminative model?

options.text: "Can you create a new example that looks like this?"
"Where in this image is the object located?"

exercises.explain: Yes! Generative models create new examples instead of predicting labels for existing ones.

cards.title: An art studio that never repeats itself
cards.body: Imagine a studio that has studied a million paintings and can now produce an entirely new one — in a consistent style, with believable brushwork — that no human ever put on canvas. That's not science fiction; it's exactly what a trained generative model does with images.

cards.title: What generative models produce
cards.body: A trained generative model can output many kinds of brand-new content: new faces that belong to no real person, new artwork in a learned style, new product photos without a physical photoshoot, and new synthetic training data to hand to other models.

cards.title: A musician who never plays the same song twice
cards.body: Think of a jazz musician who has listened to thousands of recordings. They can now improvise a melody nobody has ever played — it's unmistakably jazz, built from everything they've absorbed, yet it's a genuinely new performance, not a note-for-note replay of any song they heard.

cards.title: Product photos without a photoshoot
cards.body: An online store trains a generative model on thousands of studio photos of its products. Afterward, it can generate new product images — different angles, lighting, and backgrounds — without booking a single new photoshoot.

cards.title: It's not a photocopier
cards.body: A common myth is that a generative model just stores training images and remixes pieces of them like a collage. In reality, a well-trained one compresses the entire pattern of the dataset into its parameters and produces genuinely novel combinations — not a copy-paste of files it memorized.

left.text:
New faces for avatars
New artwork in a learned style
New product photography

right.text:
Synthetic minority-class images
Recall Unit 128's weighted loss?
This manufactures new examples instead

exercises.prompt: Which of these is an example of a generative model's output?

options.text: A brand-new synthetic face image that doesn't belong to any real person
A predicted label of "spam" or "not spam" for an email
A bounding box drawn around a detected pedestrian
A segmentation mask outlining a tumor in a scan

exercises.explain: Correct! Only the new synthetic face is genuinely created data — the others are predictions about existing input.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Generative models can be used to create additional synthetic images for a rare class in an imbalanced dataset.
exercises.explain: True. Instead of only reweighting the loss (Unit 128), a generative model can manufacture entirely new minority-class examples.

exercises.sentence: A well-trained generative model produces ___ examples, not exact copies of its training data.
exercises.explain: Generative models aim to generalize the pattern of the data, producing genuinely new examples rather than memorized copies.

exercises.prompt: Which output could only have come from a generative model, not a discriminative one?

options.text: A newly synthesized product photo showing an item from an angle it was never physically photographed at
A confidence score of 92% that a photo contains a dog
A cropped region of an image flagged as containing text

exercises.explain: Correct! Creating a photo from an angle that never existed requires generating new content, not just judging existing content.

exercises.prompt: A hospital has only 40 real photos of a rare skin condition — far too few to train a reliable classifier, even with Unit 128's weighted loss. A colleague suggests training a generative model on those 40 photos to help. What is the most accurate expectation?

options.text: A generative model could learn the visual pattern of the condition and synthesize additional plausible training photos to supplement the tiny real dataset
A generative model would simply relabel the 40 existing photos with higher confidence scores
A generative model cannot be trained on fewer than 1,000 images under any circumstances
A generative model would automatically detect the condition in new patients without any further training

exercises.explain: Exactly! Generating new, plausible examples is a direct way to address data scarcity. Next: how does a model actually learn to create something new in the first place?
